name: Grilled Fish Salad on Bruschetta
recipeIngredient: 1 bunch fresh basil leaves, roughly chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove stem, seeds, and white ribs from the bell pepper; cut into quarters.
2. Cut squashes and eggplant lengthwise into 1/4-inch thick slices or sticks.
3. Use more basil than parsley, Italian preferred.
4. Select 3 to 4 ripe but firm tomatoes.
5. Heat a charcoal or gas grill or ridged stovetop griddle until very hot.
6. Dip a towel in olive oil and rub onto the grids.
7. Lightly brush the fish fillet and all sliced vegetables with oil.
8. Grill the fish fillet just until cooked through, revealing flaky structure.
9. Remove the fish from the grill with a spatula and set aside to cool.
10. Grill the veggies until visible grill marks appear and they soften.
11. Remove the veggies from the grill with a spatula or tongs and set aside to cool.
12. When the fish is cool enough to handle, pull it apart into small pieces and remove any bones.
13. Cut the pepper into thin strips.
14. In a medium bowl, combine the fish, pepper strips, basil, parsley, minced garlic, extra-virgin olive oil, lemon juice, and salt and pepper to taste. Cover with a kitchen towel and set aside to marinate at room temperature.
15. Toss zucchini and eggplant in a small bowl; set aside.
16. Just before serving, grill the tomatoes until soft and transfer them to a plate.
17. Grill bread slices on both sides.
18. Rub each slice of bread with the whole garlic clove and drizzle with oil.
19. Flatten a tomato half onto each slice of bread.
20. Place bruschetta on a large platter; top each piece with zucchini and eggplant strips and spoon on fish salad.
21. Garnish with chopped parsley.
22. Drizzle with extra-virgin olive oil.
23. Serve with lots of napkins.
